comment: Refreshed under the exact premise-source provenance prompt. Every audit-premise is judged independently where present.
prompt_summary: For each Assumptions.lean declaration, decide whether it is an explicit paper/model assumption, a source theorem condition, a documented caveat, a partial-formalization boundary, not a paper assumption, or uncertain.
For every exact -- audit-premise entry, give an independent premise_judgments entry. Group-level approval is insufficient.
Use source_text_model_primitive, source_text, or paper_condition only for premises explicitly stated by the source; cite source_location.
Use derived_from_source_primitives only when the Lean development derives the premise from prior source primitives.

Displayed formulas, capacity equations, threshold identities, density/mass rows, source-row packages, certificates, and proof conveniences are not paper assumptions unless the source explicitly assumes them; otherwise mark partial_boundary or not_paper_assumption.

assumption_nonnegative_bids.reason: The AdWords model treats bids as monetary bid/revenue quantities for assigning queries to bidders.
assumption_nonnegative_bids.source_location: Problem statement and Section 2 AdWords model

hbid : I.NonnegativeBids.source_location: Section 2: each bidder specifies a bid c_iq and assignment earns revenue c_iq
hbid : I.NonnegativeBids.reason: Bids are source monetary quantities/revenues. The Lean predicate records their nonnegative monetary domain.

assumption_full_distinct_query_history.reason: The finite Lean endpoint represents the online sequence as a complete enumeration of finitely many query events.
assumption_full_distinct_query_history.source_location: Section 2 query sequence q_1, q_2, ..., q_M; finite Theorem 8 accounting surface

hnodup : history.Nodup.reason: The Lean finite type treats query occurrences as distinct events and `history` as their enumeration; nodup is a representation invariant of that source sequence.

hcover : AdWordsInstance.historyFinset h.reason: The cover condition states that the Lean history enumerates all finite query events in the source sequence.

assumption_epsilon_range.reason: The finite explicit-error theorem uses epsilon as the small-bids fraction/error parameter; the displayed finite bound is stated on the normalized small-bids range.

hepsilon : 0 ≤ epsilon.reason: A small-bids fraction/error parameter is nonnegative.

hepsilon_le_one : epsilon ≤ 1.reason: The finite Lean bound restricts to the normalized epsilon range; the limiting theorem uses arbitrarily small epsilon.

assumption_alive_bidder_predicate.reason: Section 6 explicitly defines the next-highest bid variant over alive bidders.

alive : Advertiser → Query → Prop.source_location: Section 6: alive bidders, i.e. bidders who still have money
alive : Advertiser → Query → Prop.reason: The predicate is the Lean representation of the source's alive-bidder condition.

assumption_next_highest_all_small_bids.reason: The all-bidders next-price endpoint applies Theorem 8 to the next-price effective charges, so those effective charges must satisfy the same small-bids condition.
assumption_next_highest_all_small_bids.source_location: Section 6 next-highest-bid extension via effective bids

hnext_small : ∀ a q, section6_next_highe.source_location: Section 6 next-highest-bid extension and Theorem 8 small-bids assumption
hnext_small : ∀ a q, section6_next_highe.reason: This is the small-bids theorem condition specialized to all-bidders next-price effective charges.

assumption_next_highest_alive_small_bids.reason: The alive-bidders next-price endpoint applies Theorem 8 to alive next-price effective charges, so those charges must satisfy the same small-bids condition.
assumption_next_highest_alive_small_bids.source_location: Section 6 next-highest-bid extension via effective bids

hnext_small : ∀ a q, section6_next_highe.source_location: Section 6 alive-bidders next-highest-bid extension and Theorem 8 small-bids assumption
hnext_small : ∀ a q, section6_next_highe.reason: This is the small-bids theorem condition specialized to alive-bidders next-price effective charges.

assumption_click_through_rates_probabili.reason: Section 6 identifies click-through rate as the probability that a user clicks on the ad.

hctr_nonneg : ∀ a q, 0 ≤ ctr a q.source_location: Section 6: CTR is the probability that a user clicks

hctr_le_one : ∀ a q, ctr a q ≤ 1.source_location: Section 6: CTR is the probability that a user clicks

assumption_availability_predicate.reason: Section 6 allows advertisers to enter during the query sequence, represented in Lean by an availability predicate.

available : Advertiser → Query → Prop.source_location: Section 6: advertisers may enter at any time during the query sequence
available : Advertiser → Query → Prop.reason: The predicate is the Lean representation of source-time availability.

assumption_weighted_bids_nonnegative_wei.reason: Section 8 defines effective bids as actual bid times advertiser weight; the Lean theorem states the nonnegative-weight regime for preserving nonnegative effective bids.

hweight_nonneg : ∀ a, 0 ≤ weight a.source_location: Section 8: each bidder is assigned a weight and effective bid is bid times weight
hweight_nonneg : ∀ a, 0 ≤ weight a.reason: The condition records the nonnegative effective-bid regime for the paper's weighted-bid proposal.

assumption_weighted_effective_small_bids.reason: The weighted-bids endpoint applies Theorem 8 to weighted effective bids, so the weighted effective bids must satisfy the small-bids condition.
assumption_weighted_effective_small_bids.source_location: Section 8 weighted-bids extension and Theorem 8 small-bids assumption

hweighted_small : ∀ a q, weight a * I.bi.source_location: Section 8 weighted effective bids; Theorem 8 small-bids assumption
hweighted_small : ∀ a q, weight a * I.bi.reason: This is the small-bids theorem condition specialized to the paper's weighted effective bids.
